summary: Checksum-bound handoff for the rust-oracledb reusable quality split and fail-closed local Required parser.

acceptance.claim: The reusable quality workflow is split into independently observable gates with a stable aggregate.
acceptance.evidence: The verified _quality.yml has validate, core, contracts, features, release-surface, musl, perf, and fuzz gate jobs plus one quality aggregate; strict aggregation fails closed on any required result.

acceptance.claim: The local Required parser derives the workflow graph and rejects structural drift.
acceptance.evidence: python3 scripts/verify_required_local.py --self-test passed at verified_head_sha, including unknown-step, changed-condition, missing-prep-result, incomplete-aggregate, and strict-evidence reachability negatives.

acceptance.claim: The generated taxonomy matches the concrete workflow checks.
acceptance.evidence: python3 scripts/ci_taxonomy.py --check reports 72 jobs matching YAML: 2 advisory, 15 manual, 4 release, 23 required, and 28 scheduled.

acceptance.claim: The current Required projection remains explicit after the later D4 extension.
acceptance.evidence: python3 scripts/verify_required_local.py --plan derives 28 required-command records across core, contracts, features, and release-surface; later matrix/prep work did not bypass parser classification.

limitations: No GitHub-hosted reusable-workflow run is claimed by this handoff.
No driver workspace compile was started because the mandatory Agent Mail build-slot service was unavailable; parser and workflow-contract gates are the evidence basis.
